core_mechanism: a spatially-varying geometric (Berry/Pancharatnam) phase acquired by a two-state internal degree of freedom under a position-dependent transformation, which couples that internal degree of freedom to an external spatial mode
brief_reason: The abstract attributes the conversion of one angular-momentum component into another to Pancharatnam-Berry geometrical phases arising in an inhomogeneous transformation of the polarization, i.e. an inhomogeneous transformation of an internal two-state variable imprinting a geometric phase that reshapes the external mode.
